entries.title: INTRODUCTORY TRACTATE ON THE LETTER OF JAMES on james 2:7
entries.excerpt: This is the name of the God of Israel, which was invoked on your behalf in Egypt, as well as in your baptism.

entries.title: Commentary on James on james 2:7
entries.excerpt:
Do they not blaspheme that good name by which you are called?
"They blaspheme the good name." A good name is a new name according to the prophet saying: "To those who serve me, a new name will be called, which will be blessed over all the face of the earth." (Isa. 62:2)

entries.title: Commentary on James on james 2:7
entries.excerpt: The new name — according to the words of the prophet: "Those who serve Me shall be called by a new name, which shall be blessed on the earth, for they shall bless the true God" ("His servants He shall call by another name; he who blesses himself in the earth shall bless himself in the God of truth") (Isa. 65:15–16).
